questions.question: What is the principle of microarray ?
questions.correct_answer: Fluorescent labelled probs bind to specific locations on DNA level
wrong_answers: Fluorescent labelled probs bind to chromosome level
It requires live cells to detect large abnormalities

questions.question: The karyogram helps detect large structural abnormalities, the size attained is due to condensation of chromatin in which stage of replication ?
Which of the following is an example of a disease caused by a structural abnormality ?
Turner syndrome is an example of :
Which of the following is correct regarding PCR ?
questions.correct_answer: It consists of a set of repetitive enzymatic-temperature dependant reactions
wrong_answers: DNA sample-template provides the building blocks for DNA amplification
The primers are identical to the DNA to be amplified.

questions.question: What's the basis of conventional PCR ?
questions.correct_answer: Detection is mainly after the process is complete using electrophoresis.
wrong_answers: A cycle of denaturing, annealing, extension, providing quantitative results
It can be used for detection of viral load.

questions.question: How are signals produced with Real Time PCR ?
wrong_answers: Using an enzymatic reaction that produces light.
Using an enzymatic reaction that produces color.

questions.question: Which of the following is added to the process of DNA sequencing ?
questions.correct_answer: Terminator bases each tagged with a unique dye.
wrong_answers: Terminator bases tagged with the same dye

questions.question: Direct sequencing is an advantage of which generation of sequencing?
wrong_answers: They are all indirect as they require amplification

questions.question: JAK2V617F supports diagnosis of which hematological neoplasm?

questions.correct_answer: It is of limited diagnostic value in PMF
wrong_answers: It's present in the 90-95% of Essential Thrombocythemia cases
It's negative in nearly 50% of Polycythemia Vera cases

Which of the following statements accurately describes a key difference between DNA and RNA ?
questions.correct_answer: DNA contains thymine, whereas RNA contains uracil
wrong_answers: DNA is less stable than RNA
RNA is typically double-stranded, while DNA is single-stranded
RNA is mainly found in the nucleus, while DNA is mainly cytoplasmic

questions.question: Which genetic testing method is most suitable for detecting a specific chromosomal translocation ?

questions.correct_answer: To define the specific region of DNA to be amplified
wrong_answers: To denature the double-stranded DNA template
To synthesize new DNA strands from dNTPs
To provide energy for the reaction

why does a negative JAK2V617F mutation screening have limited diagnostic value in Essential Thrombocythemia ?
questions.correct_answer: Approximately half of ET patients are negative for this mutation
wrong_answers: The mutation is exclusively found in Polycythemia Vera
The mutation is only detectable by conventional karyotyping in ET
The mutation causes a different amino acid substitution in ET

Which generation of DNA sequencing technology is characterized by its ability to rapidly sequence millions of DNA or RNA fragments in parallel, enabling high-throughput analysis of entire genomes or targeted regions ?
Third-generation sequencing technologies offer a distinct advantage over NGS by providing which of the following capabilities ?
questions.correct_answer: Ability to sequence single DNA molecules without amplification and produce much longer reads
wrong_answers: Higher efficiency for short reads rather than longer ones
Lower cost for large-scale population studies
Exclusive focus on targeted gene panels
